title: AWS Demo Training
description: A minimal AWS-adapted scenario: scan the network, find a hidden telnet service, and capture the root flag.
outcomes: Use telnet to access a service
Capture a flag from a remote host

levels.title: Introduction
levels.content: Welcome. Your network has a client you can log into and a server hidden behind a router. Find the service the server is running and capture its flag.

levels.title: Connect to your sandbox
levels.content: Open the **client** console from the portal (Guacamole). All tasks are done from the client host (192.168.30.5). The server (192.168.20.5) is only reachable through the router.

levels.title: Find the hidden telnet service
levels.content: The server (192.168.20.5) exposes a telnet service on a NON-standard port. Scan it and report the port number.
levels.solution: From the client: `nmap -p1-65535 192.168.20.5` — the open port is the answer.

hints.title: Which tool?
hints.content: Use nmap to scan all TCP ports.

hints.title: Full range
hints.content: Default nmap only scans common ports; add -p1-65535.

levels.title: Capture the root flag
levels.content: Connect to the telnet service on the port you found, read /root/root_flag.txt, and submit the flag.

questions.text: What was the hardest part of this exercise?
